results.rendered_html: vīṭa n. (only in  Siddh. ) or vīṭā f. a small piece of wood shaped like a barley-corn and about a span long (it was struck with a stick or bat in a kind of game, like tip-cat, played by boys; accord. to some it was a kind of metal ball; others say it was held in the mouth as a form of penance),  MBh. i, 5050  (Sch.) &c.

vīṭa n.  SIDDH. K. 249,a,3 . vīṭā f. ein runder Kieselstein, Spielzeug von Kindern  MBH. 1,5150. fg.  5156. fg.  5161 . als Kasteiung im Munde gehalten 15,1022 . ˚mukha 693 .  VP. (2te Aufl.) 2,104 .
— Vgl. nāga˚.
